title: Multi-tenant burst allocator with soft eviction playbook
preview: Problem: High-frequency burst traffic from a small set of tenants occasionally exhausts local quota tokens and triggers hard rejects or cross-region failovers. When the central limiter or its lease-store experiences transient outages, we currently fall back to a conservative deny-all behavior that leads to large SLO...
text: Problem: High-frequency burst traffic from a small set of tenants occasionally exhausts local quota tokens and triggers hard rejects or cross-region failovers. When the central limiter or its lease-store experiences transient outages, we currently fall back to a conservative deny-all behavior that leads to large SLO violations for affected customers. Goal: implement a resilient multi-tenant burst allocator that gracefully degrades under limiter outages and sustained overloads: - allow short, proportionally-weighted burst absorption using a token escrow backed by per-tenant standing reservations - introduce a soft-eviction policy to preempt low-priority work rather than hard reject high-priority requests - integrate with circuit-breaker signals so allocator behavior automatically widens or tightens depending on downstream health - provide deterministic admission behavior during global ...

title: Add integration tests: sensitive actions emit v1 audit events (hosted + private fixtures)
preview:
Context
We are standardizing audit logging across Hosted/Dedicated/Private and gating launch on “100% coverage for sensitive actions.” Unit tests in individual services are not sufficient to prevent regressions (new endpoints / UI flows missing audit emission, missing required fields, inconsistent actor attribution)...
text:
Context
We are standardizing audit logging across Hosted/Dedicated/Private and gating launch on “100% coverage for sensitive actions.” Unit tests in individual services are not sufficient to prevent regressions (new endpoints / UI flows missing audit emission, missing required fields, inconsistent actor attribution). This ticket adds end-to-end tests that perform representative sensitive actions and assert that a correctly-shaped schema v1 audit event is produced.

Goals
- Catch missing audit events for sensitive actions during CI before merge.
- Validate schema v1 shape + required fields via the shared validator library.
- Validate actor/target semantics and correlation fields (request_id/trace_id/admin_action_id) for investigation workflows.
- Ensure parity: the same action in Hosted fixture and Private fixture yields an equivalent event_type + required fields.

Non-goals
- Building...
tags: Audit logging standardization (schema v1 + 100% sensitive actions coverage)

title: Policy engine: add auto-rollback config schema, validation, and migration for rollout policies
preview:
Context
We need to extend the control plane policy engine schema to support SLO-driven auto-actions during rollouts (pause and rollback). This ticket covers:
1) schema changes (policy/config) that describe thresholds/windows/guardrails
2) validation (static + cross-field) with safe bounds to prevent flapping/unsafe ...
text:
Context
We need to extend the control plane policy engine schema to support SLO-driven auto-actions during rollouts (pause and rollback). This ticket covers:
1) schema changes (policy/config) that describe thresholds/windows/guardrails
2) validation (static + cross-field) with safe bounds to prevent flapping/unsafe configs
3) migration plan for existing rollout policies so default behavior is unchanged (auto-rollback disabled unless explicitly enabled)

Goals
- Define canonical policy shape for auto-actions on SLO breach, aligned with PRD + design spec and ADR-0127.
- Ensure misconfigured policies fail fast with actionable error messages in Console/API.
- Provide an explicit migration plan for existing stored rollout policies, including versioning/defaulting.

Non-goals
- Controller-side evaluation logic (ENG-4821 / ENG-4861).
- Audit event schema and event emission (ADR-0128, ENG-483...

title: Kernel auto-selection v2: per-request telemetry fields + sampling policy
preview:
## Goal
Add per-request telemetry for kernel auto-selection (v2) with a sampling policy that keeps overhead + metric cardinality bounded, while enabling:
- rollout monitoring (selection distribution + perf deltas)
- fast triage for tail-latency spikes / suspected mis-selections
- deterministic replay/debug (record i...
text:
## Goal
Add per-request telemetry for kernel auto-selection (v2) with a sampling policy that keeps overhead + metric cardinality bounded, while enabling:
- rollout monitoring (selection distribution + perf deltas)
- fast triage for tail-latency spikes / suspected mis-selections
- deterministic replay/debug (record inputs to the decision, not the prompt)

This ticket covers:
1) finalizing the telemetry schema (fields + naming)
2) implementing emission in runtime (events + counters/histograms)
3) sampling policy + controls (defaults, canary overrides, and “escalation” behavior)
4) dashboards/queries compatibility constraints (cardinality limits)

Non-goals:
- storing prompts or token content (PII/security)
- end-to-end dashboards (tracked elsewhere; this ticket provides the data contract)

## Context
Kernel auto-selection v2 makes a decision keyed on model family/architecture, seq-lengt...

title: Top cost drivers service: compute ‘what changed’ deltas + explainability strings
preview:
Build the backend computation for Usage Insights “Top cost drivers” and “What changed?” views.

We need deterministic, non-misleading explanations for cost changes for a given scope (workspace/project, time range, filters), primarily broken down by (route, model bucket), with additional drivers (tokens in/out, cache...
text:
Build the backend computation for Usage Insights “Top cost drivers” and “What changed?” views.

We need deterministic, non-misleading explanations for cost changes for a given scope (workspace/project, time range, filters), primarily broken down by (route, model bucket), with additional drivers (tokens in/out, cache hit rate, retries, fallbacks, streaming overhead, context length distribution, latency-correlated spend).

Deliverables:
- Heuristic definitions + guardrails (thresholds, confidence, suppression rules)
- Driver ranking algorithm (contribution to $ delta)
- Explainability strings (customer-facing copy templates + evidence metrics)
- Experiments/feature flags for tuning thresholds & copy
- Unit tests + fixtures for common scenarios

Primary consumer: Usage Insights Query API (ENG-3814) and Console UI cards (DES-913 / ENG-3815).

Links:
- Brainstorm: sources/google_drive/shar...

title: Usage Insights: cardinality guardrails + sampling for route/model breakdowns
preview:
Problem
Usage Insights requires grouping usage/cost by route + model. Without guardrails, the route dimension can explode (e.g., untemplated IDs, user-defined paths, OpenAI-compat proxies), driving:
- warehouse costs (scan + group-by)
- slow/timeout queries for 7–30d ranges
- noisy/misleading “top drivers” (long tai...
text:
Problem
Usage Insights requires grouping usage/cost by route + model. Without guardrails, the route dimension can explode (e.g., untemplated IDs, user-defined paths, OpenAI-compat proxies), driving:
- warehouse costs (scan + group-by)
- slow/timeout queries for 7–30d ranges
- noisy/misleading “top drivers” (long tail dominates)
- ingestion issues (high-cardinality tags)

This ticket implements guardrails across ingestion/aggregation/query to keep cardinality bounded while preserving useful reporting.

Goals
1) Prevent route dimension explosion in the aggregated dataset and query service.
2) Handle untemplated or unknown paths safely (bucket + visibility).
3) Cap query complexity so the console remains interactive (and export remains reliable).
4) Add alerts so we detect regressions early (new route patterns, normalization failures, abusive queries, etc.).

Non-goals
- Changing the can...

title: Update API gateway timeouts/retries and circuit breakers so failover doesn’t amplify thundering herd
preview:
Context
During regional impairment + automated routing failover, our current gateway behavior can multiply load:
- clients retry (often aggressively) on timeouts/5xx
- gateway retries upstream on a subset of failures
- routing may redirect to another region while the original region is partially impaired

Net: thund...
text:
Context
During regional impairment + automated routing failover, our current gateway behavior can multiply load:
- clients retry (often aggressively) on timeouts/5xx
- gateway retries upstream on a subset of failures
- routing may redirect to another region while the original region is partially impaired

Net: thundering herd risk and cascading overload on the target region, plus extended tail latency while the system is trying to be “helpful”. This is a recurring pattern in incidents and showed up during rehearsals as elevated retries + timeouts right when failover begins.

Goal
Tune gateway retry/timeouts + circuit breaker behavior so the gateway fails fast and predictably during impairment, and so we shed load in a way that cooperates with routing/failover automation.

Non-goals
- Rewriting client SDK retry behavior (tracked separately in docs/SDK follow-ups)
- Changing customer SL...

title: Marketplace entitlement verification API (AWS/GCP) for subscription → org provisioning flow
preview:
## Context
Marketplace buyers currently hit a confusing failure mode in the first 30–90 minutes after subscription: Redwood Console can’t reliably determine whether the customer is entitled (and to what SKU limits), especially when entitlement propagation is delayed or when the customer already has a Redwood org.

T...
text:
## Context
Marketplace buyers currently hit a confusing failure mode in the first 30–90 minutes after subscription: Redwood Console can’t reliably determine whether the customer is entitled (and to what SKU limits), especially when entitlement propagation is delayed or when the customer already has a Redwood org.

This ticket implements/extends control-plane API endpoints used by the marketplace onboarding entrypoint to:
1) verify a buyer’s AWS/GCP marketplace subscription is active and mapped to a Redwood SKU
2) return a stable entitlement object (or a clear, retryable error) that Console + provisioning can act on
3) support edge cases (existing org linking, multiple subscriptions, delayed propagation)

This is a critical dependency for the marketplace refresh launch (time-to-first-token + reduced support escalations).

## Goals / Non-goals
### Goals
- Provide a single verification A...

title: Route normalization library (templating IDs) shared by Gateway + Telemetry
preview:
Problem
- Usage Insights requires cost/usage breakdown by API route. Today we emit inconsistent route strings depending on where the event is produced (gateway vs downstream services) and whether a request hit OpenAI-compat vs Redwood-native endpoints.
- Unnormalized paths with IDs (e.g. /v1/workspaces/ws_abc123/pro...
text:
Problem
- Usage Insights requires cost/usage breakdown by API route. Today we emit inconsistent route strings depending on where the event is produced (gateway vs downstream services) and whether a request hit OpenAI-compat vs Redwood-native endpoints.
- Unnormalized paths with IDs (e.g. /v1/workspaces/ws_abc123/projects/prj_9f.../keys/key_...) explode cardinality and make aggregations expensive/unreliable.

Goal
- Ship a single, shared route normalization implementation used by both:
 1) API Gateway request metering/telemetry emitters
 2) Telemetry ingestion/aggregation jobs (as a fallback / validation layer)
- Normalize routes by templating path params (IDs) and standardizing prefixes so aggregation keys are stable.
- Provide tests + a migration/rollout plan to avoid breaking billing reconciliation and existing dashboards.

Non-goals (Phase 1)
- Not a general-purpose URL parser for ...

title: Clarify token-source attribution and recovery after shard replay
preview: Summary: During a recent audit of reconstructed usage we observed persistent per-model attribution oscillations after shard replays and targeted backfills. The symptom is that daily peak order and per-model top lists shift unexpectedly after backfill windows complete. Root cause appears to be non-deterministic attri...
text: Summary: During a recent audit of reconstructed usage we observed persistent per-model attribution oscillations after shard replays and targeted backfills. The symptom is that daily peak order and per-model top lists shift unexpectedly after backfill windows complete. Root cause appears to be non-deterministic attribution when rate-limit retry fan-in and replay markers are present: downstream aggregators sometimes favor the replay event's model_alias (or lose the original_model_id) resulting in charge redistribution to 'fallback' model names in per-model charts.

title: Clockless anchoring reset for tenant-granular quotas
preview: Problem: Tenant quota resets across daily/monthly and rolling windows are fragile in the presence of clock skew, cross-region failover, DST leaps, and reserved capacity handoffs. Existing epoch-based resets rely on UTC or tenant TZ anchors which complicates reserved pool migrations and produces brief double-counting...
text:
Problem: Tenant quota resets across daily/monthly and rolling windows are fragile in the presence of clock skew, cross-region failover, DST leaps, and reserved capacity handoffs. Existing epoch-based resets rely on UTC or tenant TZ anchors which complicates reserved pool migrations and produces brief double-counting or missed resets for high-throughput tenants.

Goal: Implement a clockless anchoring reset mechanism that uses distributed checkpoints and idempotent reset tokens to produce deterministic, idempotent quota resets for tenant-scoped meters. The mechanism should work for both rolling and fixed-window quotas, be compatible with reserved-capacity leases, and allow fast safe cutovers during migrations.

Approach summary:
- Checkpoint epochs: persist lightweight reset checkpoints to the usage ledger as monotonic markers (reset_token). Checkpoints are produced by the lease owner f...

title: Adaptive setpoint autoscale soak probe (reverse-sawtooth + steady-state)
preview: Create and validate an adaptive 'setpoint' autoscale soak probe that exercises Dedicated capacity under a compound workload: long steady-state baseline plus periodic reverse-sawtooth bursts that ramp down quickly then sustain mid-level traffic. The harness should measure tokens/sec, p95/p99 generation latency, cold-...
text: Create and validate an adaptive 'setpoint' autoscale soak probe that exercises Dedicated capacity under a compound workload: long steady-state baseline plus periodic reverse-sawtooth bursts that ramp down quickly then sustain mid-level traffic. The harness should measure tokens/sec, p95/p99 generation latency, cold-start incidents, and KV cache hit ratio; detect regressions vs a rolling baseline; and produce signals suitable for integration with our autoscaler fuzzing and alerting pipelines. Unlike previous burst/soak harnesses, this probe uses a thermostatic controller to nudge target capacity when p95 drifts above configurable thresholds, emulating an operator-adjusted with hysteresis to avoid oscillation.

title: Run KMS outage game day (timeout/throttling/AccessDenied) + capture follow-ups
preview:
## Goal
Validate end-to-end behavior and operator readiness when KMS is degraded or misconfigured across Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployment modes.

We want to confirm:
- KMS wrapper timeouts/retry budgets/circuit breaker prevent retry storms + cascading failures
- Error taxonomy is consistent (logs/metrics), and on...
text:
## Goal
Validate end-to-end behavior and operator readiness when KMS is degraded or misconfigured across Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployment modes.

We want to confirm:
- KMS wrapper timeouts/retry budgets/circuit breaker prevent retry storms + cascading failures
- Error taxonomy is consistent (logs/metrics), and on-call can quickly triage
- Dashboards/alerts are actionable (not noisy) and point to correct remediation
- Runbooks cover the most common failure modes with concrete steps
- Private installer preflight checks catch the “obvious” misconfigs before customers hit runtime failures

## Scope / systems under test
- Control plane: key version config/dual-read cutover flags
- Data plane: envelope decrypt path (runtime)
- Observability: KMS dependency health dashboard + alerts
- Private installer: KMS preflight validation (where available)

References:
- Confluence game day doc: sou...
tags: Security & Compliance Foundation: KMS integration hardening + rotation docs

title: Attention kernel choosing safety-net and synthetic benchmark simulator
preview: Problem: We continue to see intermittent latency regressions and memory spikes when the kernel selector flips between FlashAttention v2 and v3 at certain batch/sequence-shape combinations during prefill and decode. Root cause is unclear across cases: some relate to v3 kernel workspace fragmentation, others to startu...
text:
Problem: We continue to see intermittent latency regressions and memory spikes when the kernel selector flips between FlashAttention v2 and v3 at certain batch/sequence-shape combinations during prefill and decode. Root cause is unclear across cases: some relate to v3 kernel workspace fragmentation, others to startup jitter when speculative warmup probes run.

Goal: Build a synthetic benchmark + simulator that can: 1) reliably reproduce the shape/occupancy conditions that trigger regressions, 2) exercise the kernel selection logic (including hysteresis, gating, and fallback), and 3) produce a set of safety thresholds and automatic rollback triggers for staged rollouts. The simulator should be lightweight, deterministic for CI, and capable of running on CI/GHA runners and on an engineering GPU pool.

Scope: implement workload generator, kernel selector harness (pluggable for v2/v3/quan...

title: Quantization load guardrails: qprofile-audit harness for perf/quality tradeoffs
preview: Build a reproducible, auditable benchmarking harness (qprofile-audit) to evaluate FP16/INT8/INT4 quant profiles across representative workloads and detect regressions in tokens/sec, p95 latency, and output quality. The goal is an actionable guardrail that allows model onboarding and automatic rollbacks if a quant pr...
text: Build a reproducible, auditable benchmarking harness (qprofile-audit) to evaluate FP16/INT8/INT4 quant profiles across representative workloads and detect regressions in tokens/sec, p95 latency, and output quality. The goal is an actionable guardrail that allows model onboarding and automatic rollbacks if a quant profile causes unacceptable quality or latency regressions for production workloads. This ticket covers harness design, dataset selection, statistical detection rules, CI integration, dashboards, and a small pilot run with 2 models.

title: Dynamic SLI onboarding and traffic escrow policy for prioritized conversational endpoints
preview: Background: As we onboard more conversational routes and tuned model variants, teams need a predictable way to declare SLIs and safely migrate traffic without risking noisy error-budget burn across regions. This ticket defines a prescriptive onboarding flow that (1) creates per-model SLIs from sampled traffic and sy...
text:
Background: As we onboard more conversational routes and tuned model variants, teams need a predictable way to declare SLIs and safely migrate traffic without risking noisy error-budget burn across regions. This ticket defines a prescriptive onboarding flow that (1) creates per-model SLIs from sampled traffic and synthetic probe signals, (2) allocates an "traffic escrow" (error-budget currency) to new model variants, and (3) wires an automated regional failover choreography tied to the escrow balance and circuit-breaker state. The goal is to reduce rollouts that unexpectedly exhaust global error budgets and to enable short-lived experiments with automatic safe ejection.

Scope:
- Define canonical SLI signals for conversational endpoints (latency P95/P99, request-quality pass rate, token-error-rate, downstream HTTP error rate).
- Specify sampling rates and synthetic probe cadence for i...

title: Disconnected-client heuristics and partial-output fanout cleanup
preview: Problem: When clients disconnect mid-generation we occasionally see duplicated partial payloads, lingering per-generation state in memory, and cross-tenant KV cache pollution during 'fanout' (when a single generation is mirrored to multiple downstream consumers). This causes elevated memory usage, confusing logs tha...
text:
Problem: When clients disconnect mid-generation we occasionally see duplicated partial payloads, lingering per-generation state in memory, and cross-tenant KV cache pollution during 'fanout' (when a single generation is mirrored to multiple downstream consumers). This causes elevated memory usage, confusing logs that look like duplicate outputs, and complicates function/tool-call coordination for resumed sessions.

Goal: Implement a deterministic, idempotent cleanup and resume protocol that: (1) detects and cordons disconnected clients quickly, (2) marks partial generations with a compact emission fingerprint (EID) to prevent duplicate downstream fanout, (3) quarantines orphaned generation state in a TTL-based soft-quarantine for safe rollback/inspection, and (4) exposes clear metrics and alerts for stale-generation accumulation.

Constraints: Must be backward-compatible with existing...

title: CI: reproducible model release flow with signed artifacts and profile promotion
preview: Implement a reproducible CI/CD flow that produces versioned model artifacts, cryptographically signs the build outputs and performance profiles, and promotes artifacts through staging -> canary -> prod with automated checks. The goal is to remove manual steps in onboarding new model weights and their associated perf...
text: Implement a reproducible CI/CD flow that produces versioned model artifacts, cryptographically signs the build outputs and performance profiles, and promotes artifacts through staging -> canary -> prod with automated checks. The goal is to remove manual steps in onboarding new model weights and their associated performance profiles, provide verifiable provenance for sensitive customer deployments, and enable safe, auditable rollouts. Key pieces: 1) deterministic packaging of model artifacts + manifest (weights, tokenizer, quantization metadata, build environment hash); 2) artifact signing and attestation (Sigstore/cosign integration + Rekor transparency); 3) automated perf-profile generation, validation and version publish to the model catalog; 4) promotion workflow (staging acceptance tests, canary rollout with health checks and evaluation harness tie-in, automatic rollback on regres...

title: Add burst configuration to Control Plane API (tenant enablement, limits, priority tier, validation, audit hooks)
preview:
Context
Dedicated Burst Capacity requires a tenant-scoped configuration surface in the Control Plane so we can:
- enable/disable burst per Dedicated tenant
- set hard caps/guardrails that admission/routing can enforce deterministically
- expose read APIs to Console + support tooling
- produce audit events for all co...
text:
Context
Dedicated Burst Capacity requires a tenant-scoped configuration surface in the Control Plane so we can:
- enable/disable burst per Dedicated tenant
- set hard caps/guardrails that admission/routing can enforce deterministically
- expose read APIs to Console + support tooling
- produce audit events for all config changes (including admin overrides)

This ticket is specifically the Control Plane API + validation + audit logging hooks. The admission controller and routing integration are tracked separately.

Scope (in)
1) Tenant model/config schema
- Add a BurstConfig block on the tenant (or dedicated capacity config) with:
 - enabled: boolean
 - max_burst_ratio: float (relative to baseline reserved capacity) OR max_burst_gpus: int (absolute cap), with clear precedence rules
 - max_burst_gpus: int (optional)
 - priority_tier: enum (e.g., standard | silver | gold) used only for fa...

title: Filtering engine for SIEM-ready audit exports (allow/deny by category/action + actor/resource predicates)
preview:
Goal
Implement the filtering model for SIEM-ready audit exports to support enterprise requirements (noise reduction, least-data export) while preserving delivery guarantees and avoiding performance regressions.

This ticket covers:
- Runtime evaluation of export filters (exporter-side) for each audit event.
- Valida...
text:
Goal
Implement the filtering model for SIEM-ready audit exports to support enterprise requirements (noise reduction, least-data export) while preserving delivery guarantees and avoiding performance regressions.

This ticket covers:
- Runtime evaluation of export filters (exporter-side) for each audit event.
- Validation/normalization rules shared with control plane to prevent unsafe/expensive filters.
- Safe defaults and clear semantics (allowlist + denylist, deny wins).
- Performance tests to ensure filtering overhead is negligible at expected event volumes.

References
- Confluence ADR: sources/confluence/architecture-and-standards/decision-records/adr-audit-export-filtering-model.json
- Schema v2 + category taxonomy: sources/confluence/security-and-compliance/audit-logging/audit-event-schema-v2-and-siem-mapping.json
- Control plane export config API ticket: ENG-5132
- Exporter scaf...
tags: Enterprise Security & Compliance Foundation — SIEM-ready audit export (Beta)

title: Orchestrator peering route propagation & session-thrash follow-up
preview: During a routine orchestrator upgrade on private deployments using cross-account VPC peering, a subset of control plane nodes lost connectivity due to delayed route propagation combined with stale session tokens on agents. This caused repeated leader re-elections, API timeouts for customer control plane actions, and...
text: During a routine orchestrator upgrade on private deployments using cross-account VPC peering, a subset of control plane nodes lost connectivity due to delayed route propagation combined with stale session tokens on agents. This caused repeated leader re-elections, API timeouts for customer control plane actions, and transient capacity loss for several customers in EU-West-1. The incident surface was primarily control plane API errors and agent reconnection storms rather than model-serving compute failures.

title: Embedding quality simulator and telemetry adaptor for offline & drift detection
preview: Build an extensible evaluation harness that (1) synthesizes controlled perturbations to create robust golden test cases, (2) runs nightly offline quality metrics across models/versions, and (3) publishes drift and regression signals into Redwood Console telemetry and alerting. This ticket contains the simulator + ad...
text: Build an extensible evaluation harness that (1) synthesizes controlled perturbations to create robust golden test cases, (2) runs nightly offline quality metrics across models/versions, and (3) publishes drift and regression signals into Redwood Console telemetry and alerting. This ticket contains the simulator + adaptor components and a lightweight orchestration job for nightly runs. Goal is to catch embedding quality regressions (semantic flips, topical drift, model regressions) earlier and provide actionable signal (metric + example anchors) for model onboarding and runtime rollouts.

title: Opaque bundle signer and staged traffic shim for profile rollout
preview: Goal: Build a hardened CI/CD flow that produces an opaque, signed model bundle, publishes a corresponding performance profile, and wires a staged traffic shim so new model versions receive controlled traffic during rollouts. This ticket covers the signer service integration, bundle metadata handshake with the model ...
text: Goal: Build a hardened CI/CD flow that produces an opaque, signed model bundle, publishes a corresponding performance profile, and wires a staged traffic shim so new model versions receive controlled traffic during rollouts. This ticket covers the signer service integration, bundle metadata handshake with the model registry, automated profile broadcast to the profile-store, and a small-traffic routing shim plus automatic rollback hooks when eval or telemetry thresholds fail.

title: Per-device performance envelopes and model-pin release workflow
preview: Goal: Define per-device 'performance envelopes' (latency P50/P95, token/sec, memory footprints) for A100/H100/L40S/MI300 variants and codify a model version pinning + publish workflow that ensures safe rollout of new performance profiles to Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployments. This ticket produces a reproducible on...
text: Goal: Define per-device 'performance envelopes' (latency P50/P95, token/sec, memory footprints) for A100/H100/L40S/MI300 variants and codify a model version pinning + publish workflow that ensures safe rollout of new performance profiles to Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployments. This ticket produces a reproducible onboarding checklist, a canonical JSON schema for published profiles, and a gated release process that ties perf profile publication to canary rollout steps and telemetry gating. Scope excludes model compression experiments (separate RFC).

title: Quant artifact anchoring and rollback ledger for reproducible calibration
preview: Problem: We currently produce FP8/INT8 quant artifacts and calibration snapshots without a single authoritative, signed record that binds the artifact bytes to the calibration inputs, quantization config, toolchain, and model binary. That makes retrospective verification, reproducible rollbacks, and automated safety...
text:
Problem: We currently produce FP8/INT8 quant artifacts and calibration snapshots without a single authoritative, signed record that binds the artifact bytes to the calibration inputs, quantization config, toolchain, and model binary. That makes retrospective verification, reproducible rollbacks, and automated safety checks brittle.

Goal: Implement an artifact anchoring ledger and CLI verification flow that: (1) computes deterministic content fingerprints for model binaries, calibration datasets, quant profiles and build metadata; (2) publishes a signed, append-only ledger record per quant release containing the fingerprints, human-readable manifest, and storage pointers; (3) provides a verification tool to replay/validate calibration results and detect bit-rot or untrusted modifications; (4) surface an automated rollback playbook when verification or accuracy validation fails.

Succe...

title: Tenant-aware fairness simulator for regional scarcity and graceful degradation
preview: Goal: validate that admission control, circuit breakers, and load-shedding policies enforce predictable SLO degradation across tenants when a region experiences progressive GPU capacity loss or dependency silence. We need a reproducible simulator and harness that can: (1) emulate partial region failures (gradual GPU...
text: Goal: validate that admission control, circuit breakers, and load-shedding policies enforce predictable SLO degradation across tenants when a region experiences progressive GPU capacity loss or dependency silence. We need a reproducible simulator and harness that can: (1) emulate partial region failures (gradual GPU eviction, abrupt node loss, increasing enqueue delays), (2) emulate downstream dependency silence/muting (redis/cache/kv proxies returning timeouts or dropped responses without raising downstream alerts), and (3) measure per-tenant SLOs and fairness metrics under different admission/policy configurations. The output will inform runtime default policies and provide test cases for canary gating of autoscaling and admission improvements.

title: Admin Workshop: Lightweight utilities + runbook-as-task simulator
preview: Problem: on-call and support engineers spend significant time running ad-hoc admin scripts and manually executing multi-step runbooks; current runbooks are hard to test and often have hidden side effects. Goal: deliver a lightweight admin utility library and a local/runtime 'runbook-as-task' simulator that lets engi...
text: Problem: on-call and support engineers spend significant time running ad-hoc admin scripts and manually executing multi-step runbooks; current runbooks are hard to test and often have hidden side effects. Goal: deliver a lightweight admin utility library and a local/runtime 'runbook-as-task' simulator that lets engineers author runbook steps as composable tasks, execute dry-runs with synthetic data, and validate safety gates and telemetry before touching production. Scope: 1) small language-agnostic utilities (idempotent helpers, safe wrappers, canned retries) for reuse across adminlets; 2) a simulator binary (iterative prototype in Python) that loads a runbook definition, simulates each step with mock connectors, surfaces side-effect summary, and calculates estimated blast radius; 3) tight observability: emitted structured telemetry events for every simulated step; 4) safety policy h...

title: Construct latency-utility frontier for stochastic batching and eviction-aware cache tuning
preview: Objective: build an evaluation harness and dashboard to map the latency-utility (quality) frontier across stochastic batching policies and eviction-aware key-value (KV) cache TTL/eviction strategies. We want a compact signal that captures model-answer utility (win-rate and instruction-following) vs end-to-end latenc...
text: Objective: build an evaluation harness and dashboard to map the latency-utility (quality) frontier across stochastic batching policies and eviction-aware key-value (KV) cache TTL/eviction strategies. We want a compact signal that captures model-answer utility (win-rate and instruction-following) vs end-to-end latency for representative production workloads so we can automatically detect and rank acceptable config variants for rollouts and automated fallback rules.

title: Pinned version I/O covenant and regression protocol
preview: Define and publish a machine-readable covenant for pinned model versions that guarantees I/O shape, parameter aliases, and a published performance signature. The covenant will be enforced by onboarding checks and the regression pipeline. This ticket scopes the contract format, tolerated deltas, validation harness, a...
text: Define and publish a machine-readable covenant for pinned model versions that guarantees I/O shape, parameter aliases, and a published performance signature. The covenant will be enforced by onboarding checks and the regression pipeline. This ticket scopes the contract format, tolerated deltas, validation harness, and the publishing flow to the model catalog. Goal: give product teams a clear SLA-like promise for pinned models that balances strictness (prevent regressions) with pragmatic adapters (parameter aliasing, safe canonicalizers).

title: Hysteresis + probe noise led to underscaling during a bursty cold-start (postmortem)
preview: During a production traffic spike on 2025-02-17 we observed a period of sustained high tail latency and elevated 5xxs for a subset of inference routes. The immediate mitigation restored capacity, but analysis showed HPA/KEDA configuration interacting with probe noise and cold-start latency produced sustained undersc...
text: During a production traffic spike on 2025-02-17 we observed a period of sustained high tail latency and elevated 5xxs for a subset of inference routes. The immediate mitigation restored capacity, but analysis showed HPA/KEDA configuration interacting with probe noise and cold-start latency produced sustained underscaling during the burst window. This document summarizes timeline, root cause, mitigations, and action items.
links: https://github.com/redwood-inference/runtime/pull/1287 - PR: add request_queue_depth moving average exporter
https://github.com/redwood-inference/infra/pull/452 - PR: keda/poller-tuning and default stabilization changes

title: Model-tailored KV-slice memory profiles for long-context Llama/Mistral/Qwen variants
preview:
Background:
Redwood's serving runtime benefits from per-model memory profiles that encode KV residency, slab sizes, and eviction heuristics. Existing generic profiles are conservative for long-context variants of Llama/Mistral/Qwen: they either waste GPU headroom or trigger OOMs under realistic long-sequence workloa...
text:
Background:
Redwood's serving runtime benefits from per-model memory profiles that encode KV residency, slab sizes, and eviction heuristics. Existing generic profiles are conservative for long-context variants of Llama/Mistral/Qwen: they either waste GPU headroom or trigger OOMs under realistic long-sequence workloads.

Objective:
Produce model-specific, quant-variant-aware memory profiles ("KV-slice profiles") for Llama, Mistral, and Qwen families that optimize GPU utilization for long-context configs (16k-64k tokens) while preserving latency SLOs and reducing OOM incidence. Profiles should be consumable by the allocator and the routing layer to enable safe fallbacks to lower-footprint variants.

Scope & Non-Goals:
- In scope: offline profiling harness, profile data format, allocator knobs (slab size, span thresholds), KV-slice residency policies, eviction weighting, and integration ...

title: Rotation scoping mismatch causing privilege escalation between orgs during token refresh
preview: Multiple customers reported unexpected cross-organization access following API key rotations and token refreshes in Dedicated and Private deployments. Investigation shows that under a rare combination of rolling rotations and scaled routing, the token refresh endpoint returned a session token scoped incorrectly (mis...
text: Multiple customers reported unexpected cross-organization access following API key rotations and token refreshes in Dedicated and Private deployments. Investigation shows that under a rare combination of rolling rotations and scaled routing, the token refresh endpoint returned a session token scoped incorrectly (missing tenant binding), allowing a refreshed token to be used against a different org's resources. Hosted deployments are largely unaffected due to a separate auth proxy that enforces org headers; the issue appears in our self-hosted auth-service path used by Dedicated/Private customers. This ticket tracks the immediate remediation, root cause fix, test coverage, rollout, and customer communications.

title: Warm-Standby Certification & Region Handoff Orchestrator (hopscotch method)
preview: Goal: Implement an orchestrator and certification pipeline that exercises multi-region failover semantics and validates warm-standby readiness using a staggered 'hopscotch' priming pattern to avoid synchronized cache churn. This ticket covers: (1) a controller that coordinates region selection and tier routing decis...
text: Goal: Implement an orchestrator and certification pipeline that exercises multi-region failover semantics and validates warm-standby readiness using a staggered 'hopscotch' priming pattern to avoid synchronized cache churn. This ticket covers: (1) a controller that coordinates region selection and tier routing decisions during outage windows, (2) an automated readiness scoring service that runs periodic probes and computes a single readiness_score per region+tier+model_variant, (3) an acceptance/test harness to certify warm standby pools before they are accepted as active failover targets, and (4) telemetry and alerting to detect degraded priming or variant incompatibilities. Context: previous warm-start efforts experienced synchronous cache invalidations when all standby pools followed the same priming cadence. The hopscotch method staggers priming across regions and model variants s...

title: Synthetic Load Validator and Reservation Autocalibration Playbook
preview:
Summary:
During a capacity incident on 2025-02-10 we observed degraded throughput across several Dedicated clusters. Investigation pointed to a combination of: (1) under-provisioned tenant reservations due to stale traffic estimates, (2) noisy-neighbor workloads that generated burst token exhaustion, and (3) burst p...
text:
Summary:
During a capacity incident on 2025-02-10 we observed degraded throughput across several Dedicated clusters. Investigation pointed to a combination of: (1) under-provisioned tenant reservations due to stale traffic estimates, (2) noisy-neighbor workloads that generated burst token exhaustion, and (3) burst policy thresholds that allowed aggressive short-term bursts without adequate backoff, leading to cascading latency spikes.

Goal:
Create a reproducible synthetic load validator and an automated reservation autocalibration playbook that: (a) reproduces the incident class with controlled noisy tenants and realistic KV/cache utilization, (b) derives corrective reservation deltas, and (c) produces safe degraded-burst fallback policies that reduce tail latency without unduly impacting throughput.

Scope and non-goals:
- Scope: Dedicated cluster behavior, tenant reservation sizing...

title: Autonomous SLO-driven traffic throttler with contextual paging and runbook automation
preview: Design and implement an autonomous traffic throttler that uses per-customer and per-route SLO slack to drive probabilistic load shedding and region-aware circuit breaker adjustments. Integrate with the paging layer to suppress low-confidence pages and auto-generate on-call runbook actions (scale, redirect, degraded-...
text: Design and implement an autonomous traffic throttler that uses per-customer and per-route SLO slack to drive probabilistic load shedding and region-aware circuit breaker adjustments. Integrate with the paging layer to suppress low-confidence pages and auto-generate on-call runbook actions (scale, redirect, degraded-mode) when mitigation is automated. Goal is to reduce paging noise during transient overloads while keeping customer-visible SLOs within agreed bounds.

title: Tenant credit-based region routing with variant shadowing and graceful hosted backfill
preview:
Summary:
Design and implement a tenant credit allocator that influences region & tier selection for dedicated capacity while enabling controlled hosted spillback. The allocator issues soft "credits" (soft reservations) based on pre-commit, historical consumption, and SLA tier. Credits drive probabilistic routing dec...
text:
Summary:
Design and implement a tenant credit allocator that influences region & tier selection for dedicated capacity while enabling controlled hosted spillback. The allocator issues soft "credits" (soft reservations) based on pre-commit, historical consumption, and SLA tier. Credits drive probabilistic routing decisions: prefer dedicated when credits >= cost_estimate, gradually bleed to hosted when credits are low.

Goals:
- Reduce unexpected hosted spillovers for customers with reserved capacity while allowing controlled, observable backfill to hosted for short bursts.
- Maintain latency SLOs for P0/P1 tenants and keep unit cost predictable for reserved customers.
- Provide a transparent, debuggable fallback mechanism to hosted and to lower-cost model variants.

Design highlights:
1) Credit allocator
- Each tenant has a credit balance updated continuously: credits += reserved_rate ...

title: Tenant embedding shift detector for zero-day workload changes
preview: Build an online detection service that represents tenant-level usage/cost/latency time-series as compact embeddings and surfaces zero-day workload shifts (novel cluster formation or re-assignment) with probabilistic alert scoring and noise-aware suppression. Goal: catch new behavioral regimes (engineered model chang...
text: Build an online detection service that represents tenant-level usage/cost/latency time-series as compact embeddings and surfaces zero-day workload shifts (novel cluster formation or re-assignment) with probabilistic alert scoring and noise-aware suppression. Goal: catch new behavioral regimes (engineered model changes, synthetic traffic, customer load pattern shifts) faster than threshold-based rules while keeping false positive rate low for on-call and product owners.

title: Endpoint behavioral SLI spec and graceful capacity-shedding guidelines
preview: Create a pragmatic, behavioral SLI specification for model endpoints that maps to observable request behaviors (short vs long context, streaming vs non-streaming) and define a multi-tiered capacity-shedding policy that preserves high-priority traffic using graceful degradation, model fallback, regional evacuation, a...
text: Create a pragmatic, behavioral SLI specification for model endpoints that maps to observable request behaviors (short vs long context, streaming vs non-streaming) and define a multi-tiered capacity-shedding policy that preserves high-priority traffic using graceful degradation, model fallback, regional evacuation, and circuit breakers. The aim is to give on-call and platform automation a clear decision tree to protect error budgets while minimizing customer-visible regressions.

title: Archiveable quant bundles with immutable hash index and safety pilots
preview: Create an archiveable artifact format and operational flow for quantization outputs (FP8/INT8 calibration profiles, factor tables, per-layer statistics) that are: (1) content-addressable and signed; (2) discoverable via an immutable hash index; and (3) safe to rehydrate into running runtimes with automated pre-apply...
text: Create an archiveable artifact format and operational flow for quantization outputs (FP8/INT8 calibration profiles, factor tables, per-layer statistics) that are: (1) content-addressable and signed; (2) discoverable via an immutable hash index; and (3) safe to rehydrate into running runtimes with automated pre-apply validation checks. Goal is to enable reproducible rollbacks and reproducible cross-region reruns of calibration pipelines while minimizing storage growth and avoiding silent accuracy regressions.

title: Tenant-aware audit data lifecycle and cost-QoS for RBAC v2 events
preview:
Background:
Redwood needs predictable cost and query performance for audit events generated by RBAC v2 and SSO/SAML admin actions. Tenant activity patterns vary widely: some tenants generate millions of admin-audit events per day, others generate only a few. Current pipeline writes all events to a single hot index t...
text:
Background:
Redwood needs predictable cost and query performance for audit events generated by RBAC v2 and SSO/SAML admin actions. Tenant activity patterns vary widely: some tenants generate millions of admin-audit events per day, others generate only a few. Current pipeline writes all events to a single hot index topology which drives up storage and query costs for heavy tenants and increases noise for investigations.

Goals:
- Ensure high-value, security-relevant audit events (RBAC changes, SSO admin actions, KMS operations) are retained and indexed for fast query for the configured retention window.
- Provide tenant-level QoS that limits ingestion cost for high-volume tenants while guaranteeing retention/availability for tenants with compliance needs.
- Introduce intelligent partitioning and coldstore spill for older data to reduce per-tenant storage costs.
- Surface clear cost gua...

title: staged-activation-and-lexicon-sync-pipeline-cleanup
preview: Consolidate and simplify the model asset onboarding path to reduce cold-start tail latency and make weight/shard format handling deterministic. This ticket implements a staged activation flow: probe manifests/metadata, reconcile formats, pre-allocate and coalesce shards when possible, and perform a lightweight lexic...
text: Consolidate and simplify the model asset onboarding path to reduce cold-start tail latency and make weight/shard format handling deterministic. This ticket implements a staged activation flow: probe manifests/metadata, reconcile formats, pre-allocate and coalesce shards when possible, and perform a lightweight lexicon sync (vocab warmup + mapping verification) before full model activation. Replace the current ad-hoc format detectors and multiple tokenizer init paths with a single pluggable lexicon initializer that exposes a stable contract for downstream runtime. Also bump the weight-reading dependency (weight-probe-lib) to the latest patch that includes async checksum validation and streaming headers support.

title: Incident retro & action plan for RBACv2 audit-log regression and SAML session anomalies
preview: Summary: On 2025-02-15 we observed a ~45 minute window where RBAC v2 change events were not consistently emitted to the central audit stream and several SAML session refresh events were dropped by the audit pipeline. Symptoms included missing admin role-change entries in the audit dashboard, delayed session invalida...
text: Summary: On 2025-02-15 we observed a ~45 minute window where RBAC v2 change events were not consistently emitted to the central audit stream and several SAML session refresh events were dropped by the audit pipeline. Symptoms included missing admin role-change entries in the audit dashboard, delayed session invalidation notices, and a spike in console-facing 403s for recently promoted admin users. This ticket captures the RCA, mitigations applied, follow-up engineering work, and verification plan for restoring audit SLOs and hardening admin controls.

title: KV-prefix: hit-rate sensitivity curve and tail-latency memory tradeoff benchmark
preview: Goal: produce a robust, reproducible benchmark that maps KV/prefix cache hit-rate to p95 token latency and memory footprint across representative production-like workloads. Unlike previous load harnesses that focused on end-to-end throughput at a fixed cache size, this work systematically sweeps cache size, prefix r...
text: Goal: produce a robust, reproducible benchmark that maps KV/prefix cache hit-rate to p95 token latency and memory footprint across representative production-like workloads. Unlike previous load harnesses that focused on end-to-end throughput at a fixed cache size, this work systematically sweeps cache size, prefix retention policy, and request temporal locality to generate hit-rate sensitivity curves and identify knee points where marginal memory increases stop yielding meaningful latency improvements. The harness must output tokens/sec, p50/p95/p99 (in ms and ms/token), memory at steady-state, eviction churn, and a lightweight regression signal for CI.

title: Signal fencing and delegated ingest proxy for dedicated / on-prem observability
preview: Problem: Dedicated and on-prem customers run remote collectors and forwarders into Redwood’s observability pipeline. Current designs either (a) place heavy responsibility on the customer-side agent to enforce tenant isolation, or (b) accept mixed signals at the perimeter and rely on downstream filters. Both approach...
text:
Problem: Dedicated and on-prem customers run remote collectors and forwarders into Redwood’s observability pipeline. Current designs either (a) place heavy responsibility on the customer-side agent to enforce tenant isolation, or (b) accept mixed signals at the perimeter and rely on downstream filters. Both approaches increase blast radius for misconfiguration, leak risk for cross-tenant telemetry, and operational complexity for private deployments.

Goal: Build a delegated ingest proxy — a lightweight, policy-enforcing ingress layer that performs 'signal fencing': validate and bind incoming traces/metrics/logs to tenant-scoped identities, apply tenant-specific sampling & enrichment, and steer signals into isolated pipeline partitions. The proxy must work in VPC connectors and on-prem collectors, support zero-trust transport (mutual TLS), integrate with KMS for per-tenant encryption k...

title: Route Sentry: route-level observability & alerting for embedding+ranker endpoints
preview:
Goal: Provide route-scoped dashboards, tracing enrichment, token-level cost breakdowns, and automated alerts for embedding + reranking endpoints so product owners can detect quality regressions, latency spikes, and cost anomalies tied to specific routes and model versions.

Scope: instrument runtime and scorer layer...
text:
Goal: Provide route-scoped dashboards, tracing enrichment, token-level cost breakdowns, and automated alerts for embedding + reranking endpoints so product owners can detect quality regressions, latency spikes, and cost anomalies tied to specific routes and model versions.

Scope: instrument runtime and scorer layers to emit route_id, request_tags (caller_id, route_name), embedding_model_version, rerank_model_version, candidate_count, kv_cache_hit, batch_size, vector_index_latency_ms, scorer_latency_ms, tokens_generated, token_cost_usd, compute_gpu_ms. Build dashboards per-route (p50/p95 latency, SLI: successful responses, rerank_precision@10, embedding_distance_drift, cost_per_request, cost_per_1k_tokens) and a cross-route heatmap for sudden delta detection. Implement alert rules for: 1) p95 latency increase > 30% for 15m; 2) cost per request increase > 40% over baseline for 1h; 3) r...

title: SLO cohort preemptor with GPU fragmentation reduction
preview: Problem statement: Continuous batching currently favors throughput but can cause unfair latency regressions for bursty tenants and creates GPU memory fragmentation from repeated small KV cache evictions. We need an SLO-aware preemption policy that groups tenants into latency cohorts and performs preemptive slot reti...
text:
Problem statement: Continuous batching currently favors throughput but can cause unfair latency regressions for bursty tenants and creates GPU memory fragmentation from repeated small KV cache evictions. We need an SLO-aware preemption policy that groups tenants into latency cohorts and performs preemptive slot retirement + opportunistic compaction to reduce fragmentation while preserving throughput and overall GPU utilization.

Goals:
- Keep tenant P95/P99 latency within configured SLO bands during normal and bursty loads.
- Improve memory fragmentation (free slab count and largest contiguous free region) to reduce out-of-memory spikes for large requests.
- Maintain end-to-end throughput within 5% of baseline for steady-state workloads.
- Provide observability and safe rollout via feature flags and canaries.

Non-goals: full KV GC redesign, global tenant rebalancer, or changing model...

title: Deprecation sweep: compat helpers, codemods, and automated changelog for SDK v1->v2
preview: Goal: execute a coordinated deprecation sweep across Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs to make v2 the default, provide engineering-safe compatibility helpers and codemods, and ship an automated changelog pipeline so customers can find breaking changes and migration examples. Scope: (1) identify all v1 surface area to be rem...
text: Goal: execute a coordinated deprecation sweep across Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs to make v2 the default, provide engineering-safe compatibility helpers and codemods, and ship an automated changelog pipeline so customers can find breaking changes and migration examples. Scope: (1) identify all v1 surface area to be removed/alias'd; (2) implement light-weight runtime shims and opt-in compatibility packages where removal would cause large customer impact; (3) author codemods for common patterns (streaming, retries, structured output -> function-calling); (4) update docs and sample apps with side-by-side v1->v2 examples; (5) add CI migration tests and a changelog automation job that extracts API-level deprecations from source and PR titles. Deliverables: - compat-helpers packages (python redwood_compat, ts @redwood/compat, go compat module) - codemod scripts + examples (jscodeshift for TS, ...

title: LLM handoff curation and profile publish orchestrator
preview: Goal: Implement an automated, auditable pipeline that takes community weights -> deterministic conversion -> multi-variant runtime artifacts -> smoke/quality/latency validation -> generates a canonical pinned version and publishes a standardized performance profile. This ticket covers orchestration and policy decisi...
text:
Goal: Implement an automated, auditable pipeline that takes community weights -> deterministic conversion -> multi-variant runtime artifacts -> smoke/quality/latency validation -> generates a canonical pinned version and publishes a standardized performance profile. This ticket covers orchestration and policy decisions for curation (safety heuristics, trusted manifests), deterministic conversion reproducibility, multi-quantization candidate generation, and the profile publish flow that feeds Console and the model catalog.

Scope: 1) Intake manifest validation (signed/unsigned handling, provenance metadata extraction), 2) deterministic conversion engine invocation and artifact hashing, 3) compatibility matrix enforcement (opset/kernel constraints), 4) candidate quantization passes (FP16, q4_0, q4_k_maybe) with smoke latency/accuracy checks, 5) end-to-end KV/cache warmup smoke harness, ...

title: Incident slate: lightweight incident artifact and escalation attachments
preview:
Background:
Redwood's escalation channels (PagerDuty + Slack) currently post full evidence bundles which are heavyweight and often duplicate information across attachments. This work introduces an \"incident slate\": a compact, structured artifact (JSON blob + small rich attachment) generated at signal ingestion and...
text:
Background:
Redwood's escalation channels (PagerDuty + Slack) currently post full evidence bundles which are heavyweight and often duplicate information across attachments. This work introduces an \"incident slate\": a compact, structured artifact (JSON blob + small rich attachment) generated at signal ingestion and included in PD and Slack notifications. The slate is designed to: surface the minimal set of decision-making facts, link to the detailed evidence bundle, and seed runbook templates and postmortem drafts automatically.

Goals:
- Reduce cognitive load for oncall by surfacing the top 3 signals (latency spike, error class, top affected customers) and immediate remediation hints.
- Ensure the slate can be consumed programmatically by runbook automation and postmortem seeding systems.
- Preserve privacy: redact PII and adhere to retention policies before any external notificatio...

title: Reduce workspace churn and improve tiling heuristics for FA3 integration
preview: Integrate FlashAttention v3 (FA3) into the serving runtime while eliminating excessive workspace allocation churn and improving tiling decisions for common sequence-length distributions. Current FA3 integration yields higher end-to-end p99 latency on some models due to repeated cudaMalloc/cudaFree cycles and subopti...
text: Integrate FlashAttention v3 (FA3) into the serving runtime while eliminating excessive workspace allocation churn and improving tiling decisions for common sequence-length distributions. Current FA3 integration yields higher end-to-end p99 latency on some models due to repeated cudaMalloc/cudaFree cycles and suboptimal tile selection for mixed-length traffic. This ticket scopes an audit, targeted engine changes (workspace reuse, allocator pooling), and new tiling heuristics driven by model profiles.
